summary: Iteration 48: Anti-Time Theft Protocol Testing - Device Binding (Phase 2) and Photo Capture (Phase 1) features verified. Backend: 10/14 tests passed (4 failures due to rate limiting, not functionality issues). Frontend: All UI components verified.

minor.issue: Rate limiting (10/min) causes test failures when running multiple login tests in sequence - not a bug, expected behavior

critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: Device binding logic (lines 56-70) correctly checks role != admin/platform_owner before binding
auth.py: reset-device endpoint (lines 283-293) properly resets bound_device_id and logs reset metadata
auth.py: device-status endpoint (lines 296-302) returns binding status for admin view
attendance.py: Photo upload endpoint (lines 657-683) validates record_id, decodes base64, uploads to storage
attendance.py: Clock endpoints return record_id in response (lines 163, 195)
storage.py: Object storage integration with Emergent storage API working (logs show 'Object storage initialized successfully')
AuthContext.js: Device ID generation (lines 84-93) correctly generates for Personal Mode only
AuthContext.js: Terminal mode check (line 85) prevents device_id in Terminal Mode
AuthContext.js: device_blocked error handling (lines 119-122) strips prefix for user-friendly message
TerminalPinScreen.js: GDPR notice (lines 272-275) shows 'Photo captured for payroll security'
TimesheetsPage.js: Proof column (lines 168, 184-196) shows Camera icon for records with photos
RestaurantSettings.js: Reset Device button (lines 653-655) with ShieldOff icon per staff member

success_rate.backend: 71% (10/14 passed - 4 failures due to rate limiting)

seed_data_creation: Device binding reset for 'user' account during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 48 verified Anti-Time Theft Protocol: (1) Device Binding - staff accounts bound to one device, admins/platform_owner exempt, reset-device endpoint works. (2) Photo Capture - capturePhoto function exists in TerminalPinScreen.js, GDPR notice visible, photo upload/serve endpoints work, Proof column in Timesheets shows Camera icon. (3) AuthContext generates device_id for Personal Mode only, not Terminal Mode. (4) Rate limiting on /api/auth/login is 10/min - tests may fail if run too quickly.

device_binding_staff_first_login.evidence: POST /api/auth/login with device_id binds device for staff (role=user), returns device_bound=true

device_binding_staff_blocked.evidence: POST /api/auth/login with mismatched device_id returns 403 'device_blocked:...' for staff

device_binding_admin_exempt.evidence: POST /api/auth/login with any device_id succeeds for admin/platform_owner

reset_device_endpoint.evidence: DELETE /api/auth/reset-device/{user_id} resets binding, staff can login with new device

photo_upload_endpoint.evidence: POST /api/attendance/photo accepts base64 JPEG, returns 404 for invalid record_id

terminal_gdpr_notice.evidence: Terminal PIN Screen shows 'Photo captured for payroll security' with Camera icon

terminal_capture_photo_function.evidence: capturePhoto() function exists in TerminalPinScreen.js (lines 12-36), uses navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia

settings_reset_device_button.evidence: Settings > User Management shows Reset Device (ShieldOff) icon button per staff (6 buttons found)

timesheets_proof_column.evidence: Timesheets page has 'Proof' column with Camera icon for records with photos

authcontext_device_id_personal_mode.evidence: AuthContext generates device_id in localStorage for Personal Mode (lines 84-93)

authcontext_no_device_id_terminal_mode.evidence: AuthContext does NOT send device_id when heva_terminal is set (line 85-86)

frontend_device_blocked_handling.evidence: AuthContext handles 403 device_blocked error, strips prefix for user-friendly message (lines 119-122)

mocked_apis_list: Camera capture (navigator.mediaDevices) cannot be tested in headless browser - function exists but not invoked
